danger_signals.visual_form: Hostile NPCs: goblins, trolls, invisible shades, flying lizards, Will-o'-the-wisps, flesh-eating slimes, giant worms, colossal spider, water norfalc
danger_signals.meaning: Active threats requiring combat or avoidance; combat mode triggered upon contact

danger_signals.visual_form: Environmental hazards distributed throughout labyrinth levels
danger_signals.meaning: Puzzle-based instant death mechanics; world-state danger from environment, not just entities

danger_signals.visual_form: Magical items and objects obtainable from chests, bodies, or merchant
danger_signals.meaning: Knowledge-based danger; certain items cause instant death if used unwisely, creating risk through player agency and understanding

danger_signals.visual_form: Transition from isometric exploration to dedicated combat mode upon NPC contact
danger_signals.meaning: Shift in world-state from safe exploration to active engagement; visual mode change signals danger state

safety_signals.visual_form: Scattered rest points throughout labyrinth levels
safety_signals.meaning: Safe zones for recovery; dream visions occur here, marking psychological/narrative safety alongside physical respite

safety_signals.visual_form: NPC offering transactions for potions and magical items using gold currency
safety_signals.meaning: Safe commerce zone; later revealed as Mordamir in disguise, subverting player trust expectations

safety_signals.visual_form: Goblins become allied faction after their king is spared by player
safety_signals.meaning: Moral choice consequence; visual shift from enemy to ally status indicates world-state change through player agency

safety_signals.visual_form: Loyal warrior companion serving Mordamir; provides assistance
safety_signals.meaning: Ally NPC indicates safer passages; represents trust and support within labyrinth

faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Possesses magic carpet, levitation spell capability, barrel-paddling mechanic

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Initially hostile; become allied after king is spared
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Distributed through labyrinth; war with trolls establishes faction conflict
Engaged in war with goblins; player encounters signal active faction conflict

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Mysterious merchant disguise; ancient dragon form (final revelation)
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Disguised as helpful merchant; revealed as antagonist and last surviving dragon
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Present throughout as merchant; crafted weapon against dragons; survived 1,000 years

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Control of enchanted water source; fountains of youth imagery
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Peaceful civilization wiped out by Erinoch city army; one dragon survives
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Vast caverns beneath dungeon; dream visions reveal their history and Erinoch's violence

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Labyrinth in active state; enemies populate levels; merchant offers transactions; goblins and trolls at war
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: NPCs patrol or protect specific map portions; enemies engage on contact; merchant provides commerce

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Goblin king spared event triggers faction shift
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Goblins transition from hostile to allied; change from combat engagement to possible cooperation

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: 8 levels (7 on NES) with increasing difficulty; environmental storytelling through dream visions escalates
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Creature variety increases; environmental hazards become more complex; narrative revelations accumulate

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Trapped Dunric encountered; Mordamir's betrayal revealed; dragon lore completion
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Exposition through NPC interaction; player learns kidnapping trap and merchant disguise; final confrontation imminent

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Mordamir reveals true form as last surviving dragon; ancient civilization conflict resolution
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Boss encounter; 1,000-year-old narrative convergence; survival of dragon race and city destruction backstory becomes active conflict

environmental_storytelling.example: Sleeping on straw beds triggers dream sequences revealing ancient dragon civilization, peaceful inhabitants of Erinoch with fountains of youth, and historical siege against dragons

Specific enemy types (invisible shades, flying lizards, flesh-eating slimes, giant worms) communicate different labyrinth zones and escalating danger; colossal spider and water norfalc signal unique environmental challenges
Goblin-troll war visible through enemy encounters; goblin king presence; alliance mechanic shows player agency affecting world-state; Ulindor's loyalty signals Mordamir's influence
Helpful merchant NPC throughout journey later revealed as Mordamir in disguise; subverts player trust and communicates betrayal narrative through visual consistency masking true identity
8-level descent (7 on NES) communicates narrative progression toward truth; physical depth mirrors narrative depth; environmental hazards escalate alongside revelations
Certain items cause instant death if misused; gold currency facilitates merchant transactions; magical items communicate world-state knowledge (which items are safe, which are lethal)

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Engage in combat; participate in war with trolls; patrol or protect map portions
Transition from enemy to ally; behavioral shift indicates successful diplomatic/moral choice
Remain aggressive throughout; engaged in perpetual war with goblins; no alliance option
Present unique challenge through invisibility mechanic; require specific combat or avoidance strategies
Aerial enemies; environmental variation from ground-based threats; patrol labyrinth sections
Offers commerce; provides potions and magical items for gold; moves between levels; communicates false trust
Unmasked as Mordamir; revealed as kidnapper and trap-setter; transforms from commerce NPC to final boss
Serves as Mordamir's loyal bodyguard; provides assistance; signals connection to mentor and subplot of betrayal
Confronts player as last surviving ancient dragon; embodies 1,000-year narrative convergence; initiates final battle
Can dodge, swing, and stab with sword; restricted to melee and combat-specific actions
Can cast fireball spells freely on isometric field; use magic carpet, levitation, barrel-paddling; solve puzzles; acquire items

notes: The Immortal uses isometric perspective as primary visual communication method. World-state changes are manifested through NPC behavioral shifts (goblin alliance), environmental hazard escalation, and dream narrative revelations that establish historical context. Danger is communicated through enemy type variety, lethal items mechanic (creating knowledge-based risk), and deathtraps. Political situation—ancient dragon-human conflict—is revealed through environmental storytelling rather than explicit dialogue, with dream visions providing lore context. The merchant-as-Mordamir reveal subverts safety signals, teaching player distrust. Faction indicators are primarily behavioral (hostile/allied states) rather than color-coded or symbolic.
